desc: Sanya is tropical Hainan’s flagship beach city, known for warm seas, palm-lined bays, and resort infrastructure. It works for winter sun, water sports, and nature outings to islands, rainforests, and coastal viewpoints.
hls: Yalong Bay and Haitang Bay resort zones

todo.name: Beach day at Yalong Bay
todo.desc: Swim in clear water and enjoy long sandy stretches with easy resort facilities.

todo.name: Nanshan Cultural Tourism Zone
todo.desc: Visit the giant sea-facing Guanyin statue and temple grounds on Sanya’s southwest coast.

todo.name: Wuzhizhou Island snorkeling
todo.desc: Take a boat for reef snorkeling, sea sports, and bright-blue water conditions.

todo.name: Yalong Bay Tropical Paradise Forest Park
todo.desc: Ride up for canopy walks and elevated views over coastline and resort bays.

todo.name: Haitang Bay duty-free complex
todo.desc: Browse major brands and tax-free retail in one of China’s largest duty-free hubs.

todo.name: Sunset at Coconut Dream Corridor
todo.desc: Stroll the palm-lined waterfront promenade for evening breeze and skyline views.

wifi.note: Resort Wi-Fi is usually stable, though speeds can dip during evening peaks in large beach hotels.

prac.fltNote: From Gatwick, Sanya is typically reached via one or two connections through major Asian hubs (e.g., Shanghai, Guangzhou, Hong Kong).
prac.visa: China entry rules vary by passport; check current visa requirements or approved visa-free transit/policy options before flying.
alerts: Typhoon season (roughly July to October) can cause short-notice flight changes.

sweet: November to April is warm and comparatively drier, making it the most reliable beach season.
